title: Synchronization logic for memory requests
abstract: In an embodiment, a processor includes a plurality of cores and synchronization logic. The synchronization logic includes circuitry to: receive a first memory request and a second memory request; determine whether the second memory request is in contention with the first memory request; and in response to a determination that the second memory request is in contention with the first memory request, process the second memory request using a non-blocking cache coherence protocol. Other embodiments are described and claimed.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A processor comprising: a plurality of cores; and synchronization logic comprising circuitry, the circuitry to: receive a first memory request and a second memory request; determine whether the second memory request is in contention with the first memory request; and in response to a determination that the second memory request is in contention with the first memory request, process the second memory request using a non-blocking cache coherence protocol. 2. The processor of claim 1, the circuitry further to: in response to a determination that the second memory request is not in contention with the first memory request, process the second memory request using a blocking cache coherence protocol. 3. The processor of claim 1, wherein: the first memory request is received from a first core; the second memory request is received from a second core; the first memory request and the second memory request are in contention for a first memory address; and at a time when the first memory request is received, the first memory address is cached in a third core. 4. The processor of claim 3, the circuitry further to: send, to the third core, a blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the first request; and send, to the first core, a non-blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the second request without waiting for a data response from the third core. 5. The processor of claim 4, the circuitry further to: receive, from the third core, a data response associated with the first request; and 

 in response to a receipt of the data response from the third core, send, to the first core, a non-blocking-protocol acknowledgement message associated with the first request. 6. The processor of claim 5, the circuitry further to: receive, from the first core, a data response associated with the second request; and in response to a receipt of the data response from the first core, send, to the second core, a non-blocking-protocol acknowledgement message associated with the second request. 7. The processor of claim 5, the first core comprising cache logic implemented in circuitry, the cache logic of the first core to: receive the non-blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the second request; and store the non-blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the second request in a memory buffer of the cache logic until a receipt of the non-blocking-protocol acknowledgement message associated with the first request. 8. The processor of claim 7, the cache logic of the first core further to: in response to a receipt of the non-blocking-protocol acknowledgement message associated with the first request: process data included in the received non-blocking-protocol acknowledgement message to generate processed data; send a data response including the processed data; and process the stored non-blocking-protocol invalidation message to invalidate a local copy of the processed data. 9. A method comprising: receiving a first memory request from a first core of a processor; receiving a second memory request from a second core of the processor; and in response to a determination that the second memory request is in contention with the first memory request, processing the second memory request using a non-blocking cache coherence protocol. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising: in response to a determination that the second memory request is not in contention with the first memory request, processing the second memory request using a blocking cache coherence protocol, wherein the second memory request is not processed until the first memory request is completed. 11. The method of claim 9, wherein the first memory request and the second memory request are in contention for a first memory address; and wherein, when the first memory request is received, the first memory address is cached in a third core. 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising: sending, to the third core, a blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the first request; and sending, to the first core, a non-blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the second request without waiting for a data response from the third core. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving, from the third core, a data response associated with the first request; and in response to the data response from the third core, sending, to the first core, a non- blocking-protocol acknowledgement message associated with the first request. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising: receiving, from the first core, a data response associated with the second request; and in response to the data response from the first core, sending, to the second core, a non- blocking-protocol acknowledgement message associated with the second request. 15. A machine readable medium having stored thereon data, which if used by at least one machine, causes the at least one machine to fabricate at least one integrated circuit to perform a method according to any one of claims 9 to 14. 16. An apparatus for processing instructions, configured to perform the method of any one of claims 9 to 14. 17. A system comprising: a processor comprising a plurality of cores and synchronization logic, the synchronization logic comprising circuitry to: receive a first memory request and a second memory request; determine whether the second memory request is in contention with the first memory request; and in response to a determination that the second memory request is in contention with the first memory request, process the second memory request using a non-blocking cache coherence protocol; and a system memory coupled to the processor. 18. The system of claim 17, the circuitry further to: in response to a determination that the second memory request is not in contention with the first memory request, process the second memory request using a blocking cache coherence protocol. 19. The system of claim 17, wherein: the first memory request is received from a first core; the second memory request is received from a second core; the first memory request and the second memory request are in contention for a first memory address; and at a time when the first memory request is received, the first memory address is cached in a third core. 20. The system of claim 19, the circuitry further to: send, to the third core, a blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the first request; and send, to the first core, a non-blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the second request without waiting for a data response from the third core. 21. The system of claim 20, the circuitry further to: receive, from the third core, a data response associated with the first request; and in response to the data response from the third core, send, to the first core, a non- blocking-protocol acknowledgement message associated with the first request. 22. The system of claim 21, the first core comprising cache logic implemented in circuitry, the cache logic of the first core to: receive the non-blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the second request; and store the non-blocking-protocol invalidation message associated with the second request in a buffer of the cache logic until a receipt of the non-blocking-protocol acknowledgement message associated with the first request.
